verdict.summary: Pure, behavior-preserving performance refactor of NumData::lookup in sparq-core. Splits the dominant Owned dense-cache probe into an #[inline(always)] fast path and outlines the storage-variant dispatch into an #[inline(never)] lookup_non_owned. Correctness verified: (1) The Owned fast path `(!value.is_nan()).then_some(value)` after `values.get((id-1))?` is exactly equivalent to the original `if v.is_nan() {None} else {Some(v)}` — same None-on-OOB, None-on-NaN, Some-otherwise behavior; the `id-1` subtraction is unchanged from the original (1-based ids). (2) The `unreachable!("Owned handled by lookup")` is provably never reached: lookup_non_owned is called only from lookup's non-Owned branch, and the Forked arm recurses through `base.lookup(id)` (the entry point that re-dispatches Owned to the fast path), not through lookup_non_owned. Even if reached it panics rather than causing UB. (3) Match is exhaustive in both feature states (non-mmap: Sparse/Owned/Forked; mmap adds Mapped). (4) #[inline(always)] on the self-recursive-via-Forked fn is safe due to Arc/Box indirection. Existing numeric-lookup tests (gather path, dict round-trip, Forked-graph tests) cover both Owned and Forked branches, so no new differential/mutation test is required for this no-new-logic refactor. No overclaim, no ZK/MPC/privacy surface, no hard-coded perf numbers.
